overall_summary: Constellation proposes a new interconnection service called Load-Contingent New Capacity at Existing Plants (LCEP) that pairs new generation with new load at existing generator sites, leveraging already-completed studies to speed interconnection without creating new thermal power flow conditions. Constellation argues the current pro forma LGIP is unjust and unreasonable because it does not accommodate this configuration, and urges FERC to include LCEP as a replacement rate option in its ANOPR next steps. The proposal is modeled on Surplus Interconnection Service and would allow expedited queue treatment outside the standard interconnection queue.

quotes.text: Constellation submits these supplemental comments to outline a new, reliable, speed-to-service option pairing new generation with large loads at existing power plant sites of at least the same size, and to urge this option's inclusion in the Commission's next steps on the Advanced Notice of Proposed Rulemaking.
quotes.concern: Constellation introduces its LCEP proposal and asks FERC to include it in the ANOPR next steps.

quotes.text: This proposal achieves Secretary Wright's objective "to ensure efficient, timely, and non-discriminatory [large] load interconnections" by adding paired new generation and load without changing injection rights or creating withdrawal rights at existing generation sites.
quotes.concern: LCEP achieves speed-to-service by adding generation and load without changing existing injection or withdrawal rights.

quotes.text: Any final rule on the ANOPR must find that existing tariffs are unjust and unreasonable because they fail to meet "unprecedented current and expected growth of large loads seeking to interconnect to the transmission system" and find that new "standardize[d] interconnection procedures and agreements for such loads, including those seeking to share a point of interconnection with new or existing generation facilities (hybrid facilities)" are required.
quotes.concern: FERC must find current tariffs unjust and unreasonable and must standardize hybrid load interconnection procedures.

quotes.text: Constellation's analysis identified one particular lost opportunity: the current rules do not clearly accommodate the pairing of new generator/load combinations at existing generator's Points of Interconnection that create no new thermal power flow conditions which have not already been studied.
quotes.concern: Current rules create a gap by not covering generator/load pairings at existing POIs within already-studied parameters.

quotes.text: It is unjust and unreasonable not to fully utilize existing infrastructure and proven reliability paths while new load waits for service.
quotes.concern: Failing to use existing studied infrastructure while load waits is unjust and unreasonable.

quotes.text: Where additional load, paired with new generation, can be served at an existing Point of Interconnection within the already studied parameters, it is unjust and unreasonable that the existing pro forma LGIP does not provide a pathway to do so.
quotes.concern: The pro forma LGIP should include a pathway for load and generation paired at existing, already-studied POIs.

quotes.text: LCEP Service as outlined here and in Attachment A is just and reasonable and not unduly discriminatory or preferential. Like Surplus Interconnection Service, LCEP Service is an expedited interconnection process outside of the standard interconnection queue for new generation paired with new load at sites with existing generation facilities that have already been studied.
quotes.concern: LCEP is an expedited, non-discriminatory option for paired generation and load at already-studied sites, modeled on Surplus Interconnection Service.

quotes.text: The LCEP Proposal is animated by and can achieve the goal of speed to power for large load projects stymied today by extremely long interconnection timelines.
quotes.concern: LCEP addresses the real-world problem of long interconnection timelines delaying large loads.

quotes.text: The new generation must be "commensurate with the new load" and "deliverable" only to the new load with which it is paired—no net injections onto the grid beyond the existing generator's rights would be allowed.
quotes.concern: New generation under LCEP must match the load and cannot inject net power beyond existing generator rights.

quotes.text: All such study processes must be tailored to accommodate speed to service or the efficiencies of siting at Points of Interconnection already proven to be reliable will be lost.
quotes.concern: Study processes for LCEP must be designed for speed or the benefits of using proven sites will be negated.

quotes.text: The Commission should also require Transmission Providers to clearly identify the studies for evaluating LCEP Service requests. Clear and transparent rules on the studies performed by Transmission Providers are critical to prevent delay.
quotes.concern: Transmission providers must have clear, transparent study rules for LCEP to prevent delay.

quotes.text: LCEP Service is one of the simplest and most efficient ways to achieve speed-to-service without jeopardizing reliability or increasing costs to other customers.
quotes.concern: LCEP achieves speed-to-service without reliability risk or cost shifts to other customers.

quotes.text: LCEP Service also is fully responsive to the ANOPR's directives and principles, including to study load together with Generating Facilities, given "siting a large load near or at the same point of interconnection as a new generating facility could reduce the network upgrades needed to interconnect only the load or only the generating facility" (Third Principle), and incentivizing co-location which can "ensure[] efficient buildout of the transmission system" (Fifth Principle).
quotes.concern: LCEP directly responds to ANOPR Principles 3 and 5 on joint study and co-location.

quotes.text: Because the existing generator's Point of Interconnection is FERC-jurisdictional, so too must be the load's.
quotes.concern: FERC jurisdiction extends to the load at a FERC-jurisdictional POI.

bins.name: LCEP Service Proposal
bins.description: Constellation proposes Load-Contingent New Capacity at Existing Plants (LCEP) as a new interconnection service that pairs new generation and load at existing generator sites. Because the new combination falls within already-studied thermal power flow parameters, it can be processed on an expedited basis outside the standard queue, similar to Surplus Interconnection Service. Constellation asks FERC to include LCEP in the ANOPR replacement rate.

bins.name: Existing LGIP Unjust and Unreasonable
bins.description: Constellation argues FERC must make the FPA section 206 finding that the current pro forma LGIP is unjust and unreasonable because it fails to accommodate paired generator/load combinations at existing, already-studied POIs. Not using proven infrastructure while new load waits is itself an unjust outcome.

bins.name: LCEP Operational Constraints
bins.description: Under LCEP, new generation must be sized to match the new load and may not inject net power onto the grid beyond the existing generator's rights. Mechanisms similar to surplus or hybrid interconnection would apply when load reduces or drops, ensuring the no-net-injection condition is always maintained.

bins.name: Transparent Study Requirements
bins.description: Constellation asks FERC to require transmission providers to clearly define the studies applicable to LCEP requests. Without clear and transparent study rules, the speed benefit of siting at an already-studied POI will be lost to procedural delays.

bins.name: FERC Jurisdiction Over LCEP
bins.description: Constellation contends FERC has clear authority over LCEP arrangements because the load connects at a FERC-jurisdictional POI, making the load interconnection itself a jurisdictional transmission service.

bins.name: FERC Authority Over Load Interconnection
bins.description: Constellation supports FERC jurisdiction over large load interconnections at transmission-level POIs, arguing that jurisdiction over an existing generator's POI necessarily extends to load connected there.

bins.name: Hybrid Generator-Load Rights
bins.description: Constellation argues current rules fail to define rights for paired generator/load combinations at existing POIs. LCEP fills this gap by giving new generation rights that are contingent on the paired load being online and capped at the existing generator's injection rights.

bins.name: Joint Study of Load and Generation
bins.description: Constellation supports studying load and generation together under ANOPR Principle 3. The LCEP proposal operationalizes joint study by building on already-completed studies at existing POIs, reducing the need for new network upgrade analyses.

bins.name: Expedited Queue Treatment
bins.description: Constellation asks FERC to allow LCEP requests to be processed outside the standard interconnection queue on an expedited basis, similar to Surplus Interconnection Service, to address years-long delays facing large loads.

bins.name: Streamlined Study Process
bins.description: Constellation supports reforming interconnection study processes to build on existing studies at existing POIs. LCEP would confirm that already-studied thermal conditions remain valid rather than conducting full new studies, reducing time and cost.

bins.name: Co-location at Existing Plants
bins.description: Constellation supports co-location of generation and load as an ANOPR principle and proposes LCEP as the specific mechanism. Siting new generation and load at an existing plant's POI reduces network upgrade needs and speeds interconnection.

bins.name: Flexible Load-Contingent Operations
bins.description: Constellation proposes operational flexibility mechanisms for LCEP to handle load reduction or outage events, mirroring surplus and hybrid interconnection protections, so that no-net-injection conditions are always met.

bins.name: Using Proven Proximate Infrastructure
bins.description: Constellation argues that connecting load at sites with existing, proven reliability paths is the most efficient use of the grid and that leaving such capacity idle while load waits is unjust.

bins.name: No Cost Shift to Existing Customers
bins.description: Constellation argues LCEP achieves speed-to-service without imposing new costs on other customers because it uses existing studied infrastructure rather than triggering new network upgrades.
